extracted.brandBackground: Menchie's is a California-based franchisor of retail soft-serve frozen yogurt, ice cream, and desserts founded in 2008.

leadership.name: Amit Y. Kleinberger
leadership.background: Has served as CEO of Menchie's since July 2008 and CEO of MidiCi since August 2014.
leadership.whyItMatters: Provides long-term executive leadership and continuity for the brand.

leadership.name: Tom Regev
leadership.background: Has served as COO since March 2017 and VP of Operations since March 2009.
leadership.whyItMatters: Oversees all operational systems and training programs.

leadership.name: Yogi Tanna
leadership.background: Has served as VP of Operations since December 2017, previously holding other operations roles since 2011.
leadership.whyItMatters: Manages day-to-day franchise support and operational standards.

leadership.name: Elizabeth Berry
leadership.background: Has served as VP of Marketing since March 2020, previously holding marketing roles since 2009.
leadership.whyItMatters: Directs national marketing campaigns and brand development fund expenditures.

leadership.name: MJ Kwon
leadership.background: Has served as Senior Director since March 2017, previously holding franchise development roles since 2014.
leadership.whyItMatters: Leads franchise sales, recruitment, and system expansion.

leadership.name: Diana Vasquez
leadership.background: Has served as Controller since April 2017, previously holding director roles since 2012.
leadership.whyItMatters: Manages financial reporting, accounting, and fee collections.

hiddenCosts.description: Franchisees must spend at least $10,000 per year on approved local marketing.

hiddenCosts.description: Suggested annual cost of a service contract for the POS system.

operationalRisks.title: Out-of-State Dispute Resolution
operationalRisks.description: Disputes must be resolved by mediation, arbitration, and/or litigation only in California.

operationalRisks.title: Spousal Liability
operationalRisks.description: Spouses must sign a personal guarantee making them liable for all financial obligations.

operationalRisks.title: Mandatory Minimum Payments
operationalRisks.description: Franchisees must pay a minimum weekly royalty of $125 regardless of sales levels.

riskReasons: A high-severity operational tripwire is disclosed — see the tripwires section.

notes: Average rent not found; fixed costs may be understated.

underwriting.assessment: With $250,000 liquid against a $347,492 mid-point build-out, there is a $97,492 capital gap. An SBA loan or an equity partner is required to do this deal. At mid-cohort revenue, modeled cash flow after debt service is about $44,248/mo before payroll and maintenance — a thin margin of error.

insights.conceptRationale: Menchie's is a quick-service frozen yogurt and dessert concept.

benchmark.label: QSR / fast-casual
benchmark.dominantRisk: Prime cost and throughput per labor hour. Smaller footprint helps fixed costs, but COGS + labor still set the ceiling.
considerations: Budget COGS ~28–33% and labor ~22–28%; target prime cost under ~60%.
Verify average ticket and daily transaction counts with Item 20 operators, not the franchisor.
Watch for required remodels and equipment refresh cycles buried in Item 6/Item 11.

crossCheck.message: No operating-EBITDA margin is disclosed in Item 19. The 10–18% band below is an industry estimate for this concept — validate it against real franchisee P&Ls (Item 20) before relying on any margin.

buildup.label: − Occupancy / rent (not disclosed)
buildup.note: Item 7 disclosed no rent figure — benchmark occupancy applied so the line above isn't overstated

buildup.label: − Labor
buildup.note: conventionally staffed · ≈ 3.1–4 FTE at ~$20/hr fully loaded

assumptions.detail: Classified as “QSR / fast-casual” from the FDD (Item 1 / brand description).
Item 7 disclosed no rent — occupancy estimated at the 6–10% category band.
Estimated at the 28–33% category band — COGS is never disclosed in an FDD.
Estimated at the 22–28% conventionally staffed band — labor is never disclosed in an FDD.
Utilities, insurance, and R&M estimated at 6–10% (category catch-all).
Our calculation — the disclosed top line minus the benchmarked costs above. Not a figure stated in the FDD.

insights.consultCtaPitch: These are category benchmarks applied to the disclosed top line — your unit's real costs will differ, and that difference is the conversation. Want them pressure-tested against actual franchisee P&Ls for your territory?
insights.asOf: 2026 — general industry benchmarks, refine against real franchisee P&Ls
insights.disclaimer: Industry benchmark ranges for your own budgeting and Item 20 questions — NOT a projection of this franchise's results, and not investment advice.

financialCondition.headline: The franchisor's statements don't show the distress signals we screen for — positive net worth and a clean audit. Still worth a quick confirmation against current figures.
financialCondition.context: Worth perspective: the auditor issued a clean (unmodified) opinion and did not raise going-concern doubt; revenue is growing. Early-stage franchisors commonly run losses and carry a deficit while investing to scale — this reads more like spending ahead of revenue than a failing business. The real question is runway: how the gap is funded, and for how long.
body: Why it matters to you: field support, training, technology, and marketing come out of the franchisor's budget. If outside funding slows, those are the first things to get cut.

Verify before you commit: ask for the most recent interim (unaudited) financials and the current-year funding plan; ask whether the parent will sign a guarantee of performance; and cross-check Item 20 — unit closures, transfers, and terminations are the operational tell that corroborates financial stress.
mitigants: the auditor issued a clean (unmodified) opinion and did not raise going-concern doubt
financialCondition.evidenceNote: Derived from the Item 21 / Exhibit F audited financials. Figures are extracted; all ratios and grading are computed. Confirm against the source statements and a CPA before relying on them.
